ingredients.item:
Pickled mustard greens (dưa cải chua) offer just the right amount of crunch and tartness to perfectly pair with almost any rich or savory dish. There are so many things you can add them to as well, including stews, stir-fries, soups, and curries. However, you can also enjoy them on their own as a snack or use them to complement any braised dish.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD2 (47-ounce/1.4-L) glass jars

BRINE
6 cups (1.5 L) water
1½ tablespoons salt

1 tablespoon sugar
½ cup (120 ml) rice rinse water
ingredients.original:
Pickled mustard greens (dưa cải chua) offer just the right amount of crunch and tartness to perfectly pair with almost any rich or savory dish. There are so many things you can add them to as well, including stews, stir-fries, soups, and curries. However, you can also enjoy them on their own as a snack or use them to complement any braised dish.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD2 (47-ounce/1.4-L) glass jars

BRINE
6 cups (1.5 L) water
1½ tablespoons salt

1 tablespoon sugar
½ cup (120 ml) rice rinse water

ingredients.item:
VEGETABLES
2 pounds (907 g) fresh mustard greens (cải bẹ or gai choy), wilted and stems removed
2 ounces (56 g) shallots, thinly sliced
ingredients.original:
VEGETABLES
2 pounds (907 g) fresh mustard greens (cải bẹ or gai choy), wilted and stems removed
2 ounces (56 g) shallots, thinly sliced

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) scallions, cut into 3-inch (7.5 cm) lengths
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) scallions, cut into 3-inch (7.5 cm) lengths

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE BRINE In a large saucepan, combine all ingredients except the rice water, and bring to a boil over high heat. Once salt and sugar are dissolved, remove from heat and let cool.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE BRINE In a large saucepan, combine all ingredients except the rice water, and bring to a boil over high heat. Once salt and sugar are dissolved, remove from heat and let cool.

ingredients.item: 2. Stir in the rice water and then pour into jars with the vegetables you want to pickle. Trim any bruised, yellow, or blemished sections off the mustard greens. Cut into 2-inch (5 cm) lengths and rinse well.
ingredients.original: 2. Stir in the rice water and then pour into jars with the vegetables you want to pickle. Trim any bruised, yellow, or blemished sections off the mustard greens. Cut into 2-inch (5 cm) lengths and rinse well.

ingredients.item: 3. PREP AND PACK THE VEGETABLES Trim any bruised, yellow, or blemished sections off the mustard greens. Cut into 2-inch (5 cm) lengths and rinse well.
ingredients.original: 3. PREP AND PACK THE VEGETABLES Trim any bruised, yellow, or blemished sections off the mustard greens. Cut into 2-inch (5 cm) lengths and rinse well.

ingredients.item: 4. Fill a medium saucepan halfway with water, bring to a boil, and then remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 4. Fill a medium saucepan halfway with water, bring to a boil, and then remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 5. When the water is no longer bubbling, stir in the mustard greens, making sure they are fully submerged. (You can place a small plate directly on top of them to weigh them down, if necessary. Just make sure it doesn’t fully cover the pot so that steam can escape.) Let sit for at least 4 hours or overnight. After the water cools, you can cover the pan to prevent contamination.
ingredients.original: 5. When the water is no longer bubbling, stir in the mustard greens, making sure they are fully submerged. (You can place a small plate directly on top of them to weigh them down, if necessary. Just make sure it doesn’t fully cover the pot so that steam can escape.) Let sit for at least 4 hours or overnight. After the water cools, you can cover the pan to prevent contamination.

ingredients.item: 6. After soaking, the mustard greens should be light yellow and have a strong mustard smell. Drain and squeeze them dry. Empty the water from the saucepan, and then place the mustard greens back in it. Add the shallots and scallions, and toss well.
ingredients.original: 6. After soaking, the mustard greens should be light yellow and have a strong mustard smell. Drain and squeeze them dry. Empty the water from the saucepan, and then place the mustard greens back in it. Add the shallots and scallions, and toss well.

ingredients.item: 7. Pack the mustard greens mixture and chili peppers into two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (here), and then pour in enough brine to completely cover them. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine. Close the lid tightly and leave the jar undisturbed on the kitchen counter for 3 days.
ingredients.original: 7. Pack the mustard greens mixture and chili peppers into two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (here), and then pour in enough brine to completely cover them. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine. Close the lid tightly and leave the jar undisturbed on the kitchen counter for 3 days.

ingredients.item: 8. After the allotted time, taste a mustard green; if sour, they are ready to serve, but if not, continue to ferment for up to 2 more days.
ingredients.original: 8. After the allotted time, taste a mustard green; if sour, they are ready to serve, but if not, continue to ferment for up to 2 more days.

ingredients.item: 9. To stop the fermentation process, simply drain all the pickling liquid. You can then store your pickled mustard greens in an airtight container or a fresh glass jar in the refrigerator for up to 1 month.
ingredients.original: 9. To stop the fermentation process, simply drain all the pickling liquid. You can then store your pickled mustard greens in an airtight container or a fresh glass jar in the refrigerator for up to 1 month.
